dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, something terrible has happened. People we trusted turned on their own, and they don't even remember doing it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Since {quest_origin}, {quest_threat} has twisted things beyond recognition. Those affected see {enemy_type} where allies stand. We need someone to {quest_action} at {quest_focus}. There should be {quest_item} {location_hint} that can help us understand what's happening. Without it, {quest_stakes} will only get worse.
dialogueChunks.outro: We're killing our own people and calling it defense. Help us stop this {quest_time_pressure}.

playerResponses.text: I'll find the source of this and put a stop to it.
Those people need to be put down, not saved.
This isn't my problem to solve.

dialogueChunks.intro: My neighbor killed her own brother last night, {player_name}. She swears she saw a {enemy_type} standing where he stood. She wasn't lying: she truly believed it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: It started when {quest_origin} went wrong. {quest_threat} makes people see enemies in the faces of friends. If we don't {quest_action} and find {quest_item} {location_hint}, {quest_stakes} will be lost by week's end.
dialogueChunks.outro: Please, {player_name}. We're turning on each other, and {quest_reward_hint} might be the only way to set things right.

playerResponses.text: Show me where it started. I'll find what's doing this.
Have you tried just... avoiding the affected area?
I can't risk being affected myself.

dialogueChunks.intro: We lost one of our own two days ago. The guard captain swore they drew a weapon first. Their partner says they were reaching for a canteen.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Ever since {quest_origin}, {quest_threat} has been turning us against each other. {quest_focus} is at the center of it, and the {enemy_type} seem to be pulling the strings. We need an outsider to {quest_action}. Everyone here suspects everyone else already.
dialogueChunks.outro: If we don't act soon, {quest_stakes} will collapse. We'll destroy ourselves without any enemy lifting a finger.

playerResponses.text: An outsider's eyes might see what yours can't. I'll help.
How do I know you're not part of the problem?
